macroOverview.title: Macro Hub — Overview
macroOverview.subtitle: Composite Cosmic Fluidity Score · Real-time astro signal aggregation
macroOverview.noEntrySignals: No entry signals in next 90 days — stay the course
macroOverview.noExitSignals: No exit signals in next 90 days — clear skies
macroOverview.signalCalendarSubtitle: All upcoming entry and exit signals for the next 90 days
macroOverview.signalHistorySubtitle: Entry and exit signals from the last 90 days — to see how they lined up with the market
macroOverview.noSignals: No signals detected in the next 90 days — steady course recommended
macroOverview.optimalEntryText: Wait for confluence of 2+ entry signals (turbulence drop + new moon, or retrograde end + new moon). Single signals are weaker — use as confirmation for existing fundamental analysis.
macroOverview.optimalExitText: React to high-confidence exit signals immediately (turbulence spikes). Medium-confidence exits (mercury retrograde) suggest reducing position size by 30-50%, not full exit.
macroOverview.positionSizingText: Scale position with Fluidity Score: Score ≥ 80 → 100% allocation. Score 60-80 → 75%. Score 40-60 → 50%. Score ≤ 40 → max 25% or cash.
macroOverview.strategyDisclaimer: ⚠ Strategy signals are based on historical pattern correlation and should be combined with fundamental analysis. Past performance does not guarantee future results.
macroOverview.turbulenceDesc: Astrological tension/fluidity timeline with S&P 500 and BTC overlays
macroOverview.lunarDesc: Dichev & Janes methodology — daily returns by lunar regime with 6,600+ data points
macroOverview.mercuryDesc: Retrograde volatility analysis, shaded chart windows, and upcoming calendar
macroOverview.solarDesc: Sunspot cycle analysis — regime returns during solar max vs min (SILSO data)
macroOverview.backtesterDesc: Simulate astro-filtered strategies vs buy & hold with real market data
macroOverview.howItWorksTitle: How the Cosmic Fluidity Score Works
macroOverview.turbulenceExplain: Inverted Turbulence Index from our Gaussian model of planetary transits. Low turbulence = high fluidity. Historically, periods of low astro tension correlate with bull markets.
macroOverview.lunarExplain: Cosine-weighted proximity to new moon. Peak at new moon (score 100), trough at full moon (score 0). Based on Dichev & Janes research showing +6.9% annualized gap.
macroOverview.mercuryExplain: Binary signal: Direct = 100, Retrograde = 0, with a pre-shadow gradient. Mercury retrograde periods show higher average volatility historically.
macroOverview.disclaimer: This score is a signal aggregator for entertainment and research purposes. It should not be used as the sole basis for investment decisions. Always combine with fundamental and technical analysis.
macroOverview.interpretationIdeal: Ideal conditions for bullish positions. All cosmic indicators aligned.
macroOverview.interpretationFavorable: Favorable environment. Most signals support positive momentum with minor cautions.
macroOverview.interpretationNeutral: Neutral zone. Mixed signals — suited for holding existing positions with caution.
macroOverview.interpretationAdverse: Adverse conditions. Consider reducing exposure and favoring defensive assets.
macroOverview.interpretationExtreme: Maximum cosmic tension. Historically associated with high volatility and corrections.

macroDashboard.title: Macro Astrological Index
macroDashboard.subtitle: Mapping the correlation between slow-moving planetary cycles (Saturn, Uranus, Pluto) and historical geopolitical/market volatility.
macroDashboard.turbulenceSubtitle: Historical correlation of tension vs. market performance
macroDashboard.noMajorTransits: No major transits in this time window.
macroDashboard.regimeSubtitle: Does the Turbulence Index separate market regimes? Comparing real monthly returns (Yahoo Finance) during high vs. low turbulence periods.
macroDashboard.yieldGapDetail: Low turb. outperforms high turb. by
macroDashboard.interpretationP1: The Turbulence Index does not predict monthly market direction — Pearson correlation is negligible (~-0.13), so it cannot be used as a buy/sell signal.
macroDashboard.interpretationP2: However, it functions as a macro regime filter. Real data shows that average monthly returns are consistently higher during low-turbulence periods and lower during high-turbulence periods.
macroDashboard.interpretationP3: Think of it as a weather forecast: it identifies the season (risk-on vs. risk-off climate), not whether it will rain tomorrow.
macroDashboard.interpretationP4: ⚠ Sample caveat: turbulence peaks sit on real planetary-aspect dates that coincide with already-known crises (2008, 2020), and the "months" are not independent — they cluster around ~10 events. Even when a simple permutation test marks p<0.05 (as for the S&P), that p is inflated: the effective sample is ~10 events, not hundreds of months, and it survives neither a multiple-comparisons correction nor the hindsight bias. Not proof of future predictive power.

lunarCycles.title: Lunar Cycle Analysis
lunarCycles.subtitle: Dichev & Janes methodology · Daily data
lunarCycles.loading: Loading daily data from Yahoo Finance...
lunarCycles.alignsWithDJ: This aligns with the findings in Dichev & Janes (2001), indicating that returns tend to be higher around new moon periods.
lunarCycles.contraryToDJ: Interestingly, this asset has historically seen better performance during full moon periods, contrary to standard market-wide observations drawn by Dichev & Janes.
lunarCycles.howToReadText1: The synodic month (~29.53 days) is split into two halves:
lunarCycles.howToReadText2: 🌑 New Moon Half — the ~15 days centered on each new moon (from last quarter → new moon → first quarter).
lunarCycles.howToReadText3: 🌕 Full Moon Half — the ~15 days centered on each full moon (from first quarter → full moon → last quarter).
lunarCycles.howToReadText4: Each trading day is assigned to one half. We compute daily returns in each regime and aggregate them. The annualized return shows what you'd earn per year if you only held during that lunar half.
lunarCycles.howToReadDisclaimer: This is a statistical tendency, not a trading signal. Use it as one input among many.

backtester.title: Backtesting Engine
backtester.subtitle: "What if you traded with the stars?" — $10,000 initial investment
backtester.loading: Loading daily data from Yahoo Finance...
backtester.verdictOutperformed: The astro strategy outperformed Buy & Hold by
backtester.verdictUnderperformed: The astro strategy underperformed Buy & Hold by
backtester.turbulenceAloneText: The turbulence index mirrors real slow-moving planetary cycles that correlate with macro volatility. It avoids major crashes (2008, 2020) while staying invested during long bull runs. This makes it the strongest individual signal.
backtester.lunarFilter2Text: The lunar filter overrides turbulence with "always hold during new moons." This forces entry during risky periods if they coincide with a new moon, negating the protective benefit of the turbulence signal. The effect is real but too small (~3-5% annualized) to overcome forced re-entries.
backtester.combinedText: Adding Mercury Rx (exit during retrograde) provides extra crash protection, partially offsetting the lunar filter damage. The combined strategy beats B&H but underperforms turbulence-only because the lunar override keeps injecting risk at the wrong moments.
backtester.conclusionText: Astro Turbulence alone is the most efficient signal. Lunar cycles and Mercury work better as independent analytical lenses (see their dedicated modules) than as combined trading filters. The hierarchy is: Turbulence > Turbulence + Mercury > Turbulence + Mercury + Lunar > Turbulence + Lunar.
backtester.strategyAText: Holds the asset only when conditions are favorable. When filters say "exit," it moves to "cash" (0% return).
backtester.strategyBText: Holds the asset continuously regardless of any conditions. This is the benchmark.
backtester.cagrExplain: CAGR = Compound Annual Growth Rate. Max Drawdown = worst peak-to-trough decline. Lower is better.
backtester.drawdownNote: Even if total return is lower, a significantly reduced max drawdown means the strategy protected capital during crashes — often more valuable than raw returns.
backtester.significanceTitle: Is the edge real? — Significance test
backtester.significanceDesc: We compare daily returns while IN the market vs the days spent OUT (in cash). If the turbulence signal has value, the days it avoids should be worse. Permutation test, 2,000 iterations.
backtester.edgeRealText: Statistically significant difference: the days the filter avoids do perform worse than chance would predict.
backtester.edgeNotRealText: The difference is NOT distinguishable from chance. On this data the turbulence filter does not meaningfully separate good days from bad.
backtester.hindsightCaveat: Key caveat: the turbulence signal is built on real planetary-aspect dates that coincide with already-known crises (2008, 2020). This is an in-sample, hindsight-biased test — it does not prove future predictive power. Transaction costs are included; cash earns 0%.

stats.title: Is it real? — Significance test
stats.significant: Statistically significant difference ({p}): unlikely to arise by chance.
stats.notSignificant: Not distinguishable from chance ({p}): the pattern is consistent with noise.
stats.permutationNote: Permutation test on real daily returns · 2,000 iterations · random baseline.
stats.literatureNote: Figure from the academic literature, not computed from this data.

companyDetail.peRatioTip: Price-to-Earnings: how many times annual earnings you're paying for the stock. Lower = cheaper (rough guide; <25 reasonable, >40 expensive). Complements our EV/FCF valuation.
companyDetail.discardedSince: Discarded on {date} ({days} days ago) — check whether anything has changed. Click to restore.
companyDetail.checkScoreHighTip: Total score above 70/100: a strong candidate per the algorithm.
companyDetail.checkScoreMidTip: Total score above 40/100: clears the minimum threshold of interest.
companyDetail.checkPassesFiltersTip: Breaks no hard filter (excessive leverage, insufficient interest coverage, etc.).
companyDetail.checkValuationAbove50Tip: Valuation pillar above 50/100: reasonable price relative to fundamentals.
companyDetail.checkTrendAbove50Tip: Quality/Trend pillar above 50/100: quality business that's improving.
companyDetail.checkTimingAbove50Tip: Timing pillar above 50/100: favorable technical moment to enter.
companyDetail.checkEquityPositiveTip: Shareholders' equity is positive: the company isn't technically insolvent on the books.
companyDetail.checkOperatingPositiveTip: Operating profit is positive: it makes money from its core business.
companyDetail.checkEvFcfTip: Free cash flow yield on enterprise value (FCF/EV) ≥ 4%. Measures how cheap it is vs the cash it generates, accounting for debt.
companyDetail.checkFcfAbove5Tip: Free cash flow yield ≥ 5%: generates ample cash relative to its market cap.
companyDetail.checkTangibleBookTip: Tangible book value is positive vs the market: real assets behind the price, not just intangibles.
companyDetail.checkLeverageOkTip: Net debt / EBITDA < 3×: leverage under control.
companyDetail.checkCoverageOkTip: Interest coverage > 2×: operating profit comfortably covers the debt's interest.
companyDetail.checkFcfPositiveTip: Free cash flow is positive: it generates cash rather than burning it.
companyDetail.checkEbitAbove10Tip: EBIT margin > 10%: the business is profitable at the operating level.
companyDetail.checkGrossAbove30Tip: Gross margin > 30%: good pricing power and cost structure.
companyDetail.checkRocAbove10Tip: ROC > 10%: earns a good return on total invested capital (a better metric than ROE for comparing companies with different debt).
companyDetail.checkEbitImprovingTip: EBIT margin is improving year over year: operating profitability is rising.
companyDetail.checkGrossImprovingTip: Gross margin improving year over year: better efficiency or pricing power.
companyDetail.checkRoeImprovingTip: ROE improving year over year: equity is earning more over time.
companyDetail.checkRocImprovingTip: ROC improving year over year: invested capital is earning more over time.
companyDetail.checkEbitdaEfficiencyTip: EBITDA grows at least as fast as assets: it grows without bloating the balance sheet (quality growth, not by buying assets).
companyDetail.checkNoDilutionTip: No shareholder dilution: share count is stable or falling (buybacks). Dilution erodes value per share.
companyDetail.checkCleanAccrualsTip: Low accruals: reported earnings are backed by real cash, not accounting entries (lower risk of window-dressing).
companyDetail.check1MPositiveTip: Positive return over the last month: short-term momentum in favor.
companyDetail.check3MPositiveTip: Positive return over the last 3 months: medium-term trend in favor.
companyDetail.checkNoEuphoriaTip: Hasn't risen more than 40% in 6 months: avoids buying into already-overheated euphoria.
companyDetail.checkNear52LowTip: Trades near its 52-week low: possible discounted entry (for an opportunity investor).
companyDetail.checkRevisionsUpTip: More upward than downward earnings revisions in 30 days: analysts are raising estimates.
companyDetail.checkEstimateStableTip: Earnings estimate hasn't deteriorated in 30 days: no worrying cuts.
companyDetail.checkClusterBuyingTip: Clustered insider buying (≥3 and more buys than sells in 6 months): executives are betting on their own company.
companyDetail.checkLowShortTip: Low short interest (<10% of free float): little pressure from short sellers betting against it.
companyDetail.totalEquityTip: Total Equity = Total Assets minus Total Liabilities. A negative value can be a red flag, though acceptable for large-caps undergoing massive buybacks.
companyDetail.marketCapTip: Total market value of the company's outstanding shares. Used to determine the algorithm's Tier (Large, Mid, Small).
companyDetail.operatingProfitTip: Profit from core business operations. Must be positive as a baseline health indicator.
companyDetail.fcfYieldTip: Free Cash Flow Yield: How much free cash the company generates relative to its market value. Higher is better.
companyDetail.bookToMarketTip: Equity divided by Market Cap. Value > 1.0 means it trades below liquidation value. Higher indicates a potential 'classic value' bargain.
companyDetail.yoyNotAvailable: YoY trend data not available for this ticker — showing current values
companyDetail.assetGrowthTip: How fast the company is expanding its asset base (investing).
companyDetail.ebitdaGrowthTip: How fast core independent earnings are growing.
companyDetail.ebitdaPassMsg: EBITDA growth outpaces asset growth — efficient capital allocation
companyDetail.ebitdaFailMsg: Asset growth exceeds EBITDA growth — potential inefficiency
companyDetail.week52LowTip: The lowest price in the last year. The algorithm rewards trading closer to the bottom.
companyDetail.return1MTip: Short-term momentum. Slightly positive returns are rewarded.
companyDetail.return6MTip: Medium-term momentum. Very high returns are penalized as 'euphoria'.
companyDetail.euphoriaWarning: High 6M returns suggest possible euphoria — mean reversion risk
companyDetail.insightScoreDesc: The composite score weighs three fundamental pillars: Valuation (40%), fundamental quality & trend (30%), and price timing (30%), adjusted by macro conditions. Metrics with no available data are renormalized as neutral — they never penalize.
companyDetail.cosmicCompositeTip: Weighted composite of three cosmic signals: Astro Turbulence (40%), Lunar Phase (35%), and Mercury Status (25%). Higher values indicate favorable market weather.
companyDetail.cosmicTurbulenceTip: Inverted turbulence index from planetary cycles. Low turbulence = high fluidity = favorable market conditions.
companyDetail.cosmicLunarTip: Score based on lunar cycle position. New moon periods are historically favorable (Dichev & Janes research).
companyDetail.cosmicMercuryTip: Mercury retrograde penalizes the score. Direct periods with no upcoming retrogrades score highest.
companyDetail.cosmicPeakMsg: Peak cosmic fluidity — historically favorable market conditions
companyDetail.cosmicNeutralMsg: Neutral cosmic conditions — normal market environment
companyDetail.cosmicCautionMsg: Low fluidity detected — exercise caution with new positions
companyDetail.insightCosmicDesc: The Cosmic Fluidity Score acts as a market tailwind/headwind indicator. It assesses the astrological environment, not the company itself. Favorable cosmic weather nudges swing-trade timing.
companyDetail.insightFiltersDesc: Hard filters are mandatory pass/fail gates. A company failing any of these is automatically flagged as AVOID regardless of its score.
companyDetail.insightValuationDesc: These metrics measure how cheaply you can buy each dollar of cash flow and book value. Higher is better from a value investing perspective.
companyDetail.insightTrendDesc: Evaluates operational quality and its trajectory. Improving margins and returns signal a healthy, well-managed business.
companyDetail.insightTimingDesc: Measures price momentum and proximity to key levels. The algorithm favors stocks near 52-week lows with mild positive momentum, penalizing euphoric rallies.
companyDetail.verdictStrong: This stock meets the majority of quality, valuation, and timing criteria. The algorithm considers it a strong candidate for further due diligence.
companyDetail.verdictNeutral: Mixed signals. Some positive attributes are offset by weaknesses in other areas. Monitor for improving conditions before committing capital.
companyDetail.verdictWeak: Significant red flags detected. The algorithm recommends avoiding this stock until fundamental and/or momentum conditions improve materially.
companyDetail.evFcfYieldTip: Free cash flow over enterprise value (market cap + net debt). Leverage-proof: debt sits in the denominator, so a levered company no longer looks artificially cheap.
companyDetail.tangibleBtmTip: Book-to-market excluding goodwill and intangibles. Negative = equity is all acquisition goodwill — red flag.
companyDetail.netDebtEbitdaTip: Years of EBITDA needed to repay net debt. <1.5× comfortable · 3× demanding · >5× excessive (hard filter). Negative = net cash.
companyDetail.interestCoverageTip: EBIT / interest expense. <2× fragile, <1× operating profit does not even cover interest (hard filter).
companyDetail.checkNoDilution: No shareholder dilution (flat share count or buybacks)
companyDetail.nextEarningsTip: Next earnings report date — the #1 automatable catalyst. Volatility clusters around this date.
companyDetail.epsRevisionsTip: Analysts who raised (↑) or cut (↓) their current-year EPS estimate in the last 30 days, plus consensus drift. Upward revisions are one of the best-evidenced factors.
companyDetail.insiderActivityTip: Reported executive transactions (US Form 4). Open-market cluster buying by several insiders is a well-evidenced bullish signal; sales are noise.
companyDetail.insiderOwnTip: Share of the company held by executives and directors — skin in the game.
companyDetail.shortFloatTip: Percent of float sold short. >10% means meaningful bearish bets (or squeeze risk).
companyDetail.insightCatalystsDesc: Informational momentum signals: dates that concentrate volatility, where consensus estimates are moving, and what insiders are doing. Insider data available for US tickers only (Form 4).
companyDetail.financialSectorNote: Financial sector: debt/EBITDA and interest coverage do not apply (deposits count as debt; interest is their raw material). Valued on FCF over market cap and book-to-market. For real bank solvency: CET1 and NPLs.

settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Local user configuration — saved to your disk
settings.dataKeysNote: On-chain data (holders, concentration) already works with no key via Blockscout. Only the catalyst calendar (CoinMarketCal) needs a free key. Stored only on your disk. Without it, catalysts show as N/D (neutral).
settings.privacyNote: Your keys are stored only on your local disk (user-data/settings.json) and are never sent to any external server.
settings.storageDesc: All your data (watchlist, API keys) is saved to the project folder:

screener.title: Screener
screener.subtitle: Scan a universe and let the algorithm rank the opportunities for you.
screener.firstScanNote: The first scan can take a few seconds (it downloads each company's fundamentals). After that it's cached and fast.
screener.emptyDesc: The algorithm will score every company and rank them by opportunity.

aiAnalysis.title: Qualitative Analysis · AI
aiAnalysis.emptyHint: Generate the qualitative layer with your configured LLM: upcoming catalysts (FDA/EMA approvals for pharma, lawsuits, M&A, refinancings), governance risks, competitive moat and a qualitative score. Grounded on the quantitative data already computed; cached on your disk.
aiAnalysis.disclaimer: ⚠ AI-generated with knowledge up to its training cutoff: may contain errors or omissions; verify dates and regulatory milestones before trading. Not financial advice.
aiAnalysis.emptyHintCrypto: Generate the qualitative layer with your LLM: the technology explained plainly, its real use case, roadmap and practical catalysts, risks (unlocks, centralization, governance) and competitors. Grounded on the quantitative pillars already computed. No price opinions.
aiAnalysis.disclaimerCrypto: ⚠ AI-generated with knowledge up to its training cutoff. Does not predict price and is not financial advice; verify milestones and dates. Crypto is a highly speculative market.

cryptoDetail.disclaimer: Crypto is a highly speculative market. This does not predict price: it aggregates fundamental signals (tokenomics, on-chain, development, catalysts). What we don't have shows as N/D (neutral, never penalizes).
cryptoDetail.fearGreedTip: Crypto fear/greed index (0 = extreme fear, 100 = extreme greed). Used as a contrarian macro adjustment: extreme fear nudges the score up slightly.
cryptoDetail.unlockWarn: Token unlock in {days} days — potential sell pressure.
cryptoDetail.supplyDilutedTip: What share of max supply already circulates. Near 100% = no large future emissions to dilute holders.
cryptoDetail.fdvMcTip: Fully-diluted valuation over current market cap. Closer to 1 = less future dilution from emissions.
cryptoDetail.cryptoPSTip: Market cap over annualized protocol fees. The P/E analog: lower = cheaper relative to what it earns.
cryptoDetail.mcTvlTip: Market cap over total value locked. Lower = backed by more real capital in the protocol.
cryptoDetail.tvlTip: Total Value Locked: money actually deposited in the protocol. A proxy for real usage (DeFiLlama).
cryptoDetail.annualFeesTip: Protocol fees over the last 30 days, annualized. Real revenue it generates (DeFiLlama).
cryptoDetail.devCommitsTip: Commits to the repository in the last 4 weeks. Measures whether the project is actively developed.
cryptoDetail.tvlTrendTip: 7-day TVL change. Capital flowing into or out of the protocol.
cryptoDetail.holdersTip: Number of addresses holding the token (EVM tokens, via Blockscout, no key). N/D on unsupported chains.
cryptoDetail.concentrationTip: Share of supply held by the 10 largest holders. High = centralization and coordinated-sell risk.
cryptoDetail.whaleAccumTip: Change in the aggregate balance of the largest holders. Since no free history exists, we build it locally: every time you open the detail a snapshot is saved and compared to an earlier one. Positive = whales are accumulating.
cryptoDetail.whaleHistoryBuilding: Building history ({n} snapshot/s). Come back over the next days to see accumulation.
cryptoDetail.athDiscountTip: Distance below the all-time high. Deep discounts on solid projects = opportunity.
cryptoDetail.atlDistanceTip: Distance above the all-time low. Guards against buying assets breaking into new lows.
cryptoDetail.newAccountsTip: Rate of new account creation on the network, estimated from recent CRYPTOCREATEACCOUNT transactions scaled to 24h. An adoption proxy: more accounts created = more people onboarding.
cryptoDetail.onchainSupplyTip: Supply read straight from Hedera's Mirror Node, not an aggregator. Authoritative source.

home.subtitle: Your investing copilot: serious fundamental analysis and esoteric exploration in one app. Pick where to start or review your watchlist at a glance.
home.seriousDesc: Explorer, screener and fundamental scoring (EV valuation, solvency, quality, catalysts) + VIX volatility regime.
home.esotericDesc: Astro turbulence, lunar cycles, Mercury retrograde, solar activity and confluences — computed from a real ephemeris.
